chapter.label: Tale 16
chapter.title: Abba NICOLAS’ STORY 18. THE LIFE OF ANOTHER ELDER AT THE

paragraphs.text: ABOUT HIMSELF AND HIS FRIENDS MONASTERY OF THE LAVRA WHO SLEPT WITH LIONS ‘There was an elder living at the Lavra of Abba Peter® near the holy Jordan whose name was Nicolas. He lold us that when he was Abba Polychronius the priest also told us about another elder living staying at Raithou,* three of the brethren, <of whom he was one> in the same Lavra of Abba Peter who would often go off and stay were sent to perform a service® at the Thebaid, ‘But when we were on the banks of the holy Jordan. There he found a lion's den in going through the desert’, he said, ‘we lost our way and wandered which he installed himself. One day he found two lion-cubs in the far and wide. Our water was all used up and we went for days cave. Wrapping them up in his cloak,* he took them to church. ‘If without finding any. We began to faint from thirst and beat. When we kept the commandments of our Lord Jesus Christ’, he said, we could not take one more step, we fouad some tamarisk trees ‘these animals would fear us. But because of our sins we have there in the desert and (ung ourselves down wherever any shade become slaves and it is rather we who fear them’. Greatly edilied, could be found, fully expecting to dic of thirst. As I lay there I fell the brethren returned to their caves. into an ecstasy and I saw a pool of water full to overflowing. Two people were standing at the edge of the pool, drawing water with a 19. ABBA ELDAK’S STORY ABOUT HIMSELF wooden vessel. T began to make a request of one of them in these words: ‘Of your charity, sir, give me a little water, for I am faint’, Abba Elijzh the grazer* told us that be was once living in a cave in but be was unwilling to grant my request. The other one said to the area around Jordan because he was not in communion with him: ‘Give him a little’, but he replied: "No, let us not give him any, Abba Macarios, the Bishop of Jerusalem:* for he is too easy-going,* and does not take care <of his soul’.> The One day, about the sixth hour, when the heat was at its most other said: ‘Yes, yes; it is true that he is easy-going but he is intense, somebody came knocking at the cave. I went oul and saw hospitable® to strangers’,—and so he gave some to me and also to a woman there. ‘What are you doing here? I asked her, and she my companions. We drank and went on our way, travelling three answered in these words: ‘Abba, I too follow this way of life, sir. I more days without drinking until we reached civilisation.* have a little cave in which I live about a stone’s throw {rom your cell’. She pointed out to me where it was located, away to the south.
